evidence: `src/types/generationRow.ts` is a pure alias re-export while domain files like `src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts` use direct domain-local imports, creating multiple public entry points for the same model.

`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` embeds product-specific retro policy (`retroButtonPolicy`, retro variants/sizes) inside the shared UI primitive layer that otherwise contains generic primitives (`label.tsx`, `tooltip.tsx`, `cn.ts`).

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` and `src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx` both rely on module-level singleton state/manager patterns, making runtime ownership implicit and coupling behavior to import/getter usage.

`useProcessingTimestamp`, `useCompletedTimestamp`, and `useUpdatingTimestamp` are thin wrappers around `useLiveRelativeTimestamp` with near-identical option plumbing and only small formatter differences.

`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` builds large memoized adapter objects (`lightboxCore`, `lightboxMedia`, `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxNavigation`) primarily to reshape data for `useLightboxStateValue`/`LightboxLayout`, increasing indirection without reducing API width.

`generationTaskBridge.ts` combines React hook exports (`useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`) with cache preloading and direct Supabase query utilities (`preloadGenerationTaskMappings`, `enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData`) in one abstraction layer.

`taskParamsUtils.ts` must read the same logical fields from multiple schema shapes (`orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, `individual_segment_params`, top-level), indicating boundary translation is fragmented rather than centralized.

`VideoTravelDetails.tsx` and `ImageGenerationDetails.tsx` each re-implement fallback chains over nested task params despite using `buildTaskParamsViewModel`, so UI seams still carry backend-shape conditionals.

`buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` emits a very broad payload object with many conditional branches and legacy stripping, while downstream readers still need multi-location fallback logic, showing incomplete contract consolidation across producer/consumer seams.

findings.summary: Generation model types are exposed through parallel paths with unclear ownership.
evidence: `src/types/generationRow.ts` only re-exports `@/domains/generation/types/generationRow`.
`src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts` imports `GenerationRow` from a domain-local relative path, showing mixed consumption patterns for the same type family.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical entry point for generation-domain types (domain barrel or `src/types` facade), migrate imports to it, and deprecate the secondary path to reduce architectural ambiguity.

findings.summary: Shared UI primitives layer mixes generic components with product-specific button behavior.
evidence: `button.tsx` imports and applies `retroButtonPolicy` plus retro-only variant/size guards inside the base `Button` primitive.
`label.tsx` and `tooltip.tsx` remain framework-style generic primitives in the same `shared/components/ui` layer, indicating mixed responsibilities under one ownership boundary.
findings.suggestion: Keep `Button` as a generic primitive and move retro behavior into a separate themed wrapper (for example `RetroButton` in a feature/theme package) so shared UI stays domain-agnostic.

findings.summary: Core integration and notification surfaces rely on hidden module singletons.
evidence: `client.ts` stores `instrumentationInstalled` and `clientInstance` at module scope and performs instrumentation/runtime initialization implicitly when `getSupabaseClient()` is first called.
`sonner.tsx` exports global `toast` behavior via imported singleton manager patterns (`toastManager`, `toast`) from shared UI infrastructure.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit app bootstrap/providers that initialize and inject runtime services (Supabase client and toast manager), and make consumers depend on injected instances instead of hidden module-level singletons.

findings.summary: Three timestamp hooks duplicate wrapper logic with minimal behavioral differentiation.
evidence: Each hook forwards to `useLiveRelativeTimestamp` with `{ dateInput, disabled, formatter }` and mostly differs only by label text/formatting behavior.
`useTaskTimestamp` is a second-level wrapper over `useUpdatingTimestamp`, adding another thin indirection layer.
findings.suggestion: Replace these with a single `useRelativeTimestamp({ dateInput, mode, disabled })` (or formatter map) and keep only tiny mode-specific helpers where domain wording differs.

findings.summary: Video lightbox adds heavy adapter layers that mostly reshape existing state objects.
evidence: `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` creates multiple large memoized objects to repackage values already present in `env`, `sharedState`, and `editModel`.
`LightboxLayout` consumes a broad prop surface plus multiple context reads, so the adapter layer does not significantly reduce boundary complexity.
findings.suggestion: Define a narrower screen contract (small typed view model) and move one-time translation into a single boundary mapper; let layout/components consume domain-focused slices directly instead of repeated repackaging.

findings.summary: Task param consumers rely on multi-source fallback chains because producer contract is not canonical.
evidence: `derivePrompt`, `deriveInputImages`, and related extractors check multiple parallel locations for the same fields.
`VideoTravelDetails` still manually traverses nested payload locations for phase config, advanced mode, and structure guidance.
`ImageGenerationDetails` mixes `taskViewModel` with direct deep access into `originalParams` and `orchestrator_details`.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a versioned canonical task-details DTO at task creation/completion boundaries and normalize once (ingest step), then make UI components consume only normalized fields.

findings.summary: Generation-task bridge mixes React hook API, cache orchestration, and DB data access in one seam.
evidence: `generationTaskBridge.ts` exports a React mutation hook and also non-hook async cache preload logic with direct Supabase reads.
This creates a hybrid boundary where hook consumers and data-layer utilities depend on the same module lifecycle and imports.
findings.suggestion: Split into `generationTaskQueries` (pure data access), `generationTaskCache` (query-client utilities), and hook wrappers in `hooks/` so module dependencies reflect runtime boundaries cleanly.
